string.text: %s spits to the side. 'This one is mine, stalker. You want it? %s RU. Nu, yes or no?'
%s looks you over. 'Pay %s RU and the body is yours. Nothing personal.'
'Too many scavengers in the Zone,' mutters %s. 'Give me %s RU and dig through it yourself.'
%s keeps a hand on his rifle. 'I put it down, so it is mine. For %s RU, I did not see you.'
'Everything has its price out here,' says %s. 'Hand over %s RU and the body is yours.'
%s shrugs. 'The Zone takes, the Zone gives. %s RU, and I saw nothing.'
%s takes the money and nods. 'Good. It is yours.'
'Wise,' says %s, stepping aside. The body is yours now.
%s counts the notes once. 'Yours. Do not make a habit of it.'
The deal is done. %s nods toward the body.
%s tucks the roubles away. 'Go on, take what you need.'
You cannot put together the %s RU.
Not enough roubles on you for this.
'Come back when you have the %s RU,' he says, turning away.
Your pockets are too empty for this deal.
This kill belongs to %s. Go and speak with them if you want it.
Someone already claimed this one. Find %s and make an offer.
%s brought this one down. You will have to deal with them.
Not yours to take. Find %s and talk it over.
This one is spoken for. Best go and ask %s.
the stalker
Mutant
NPC claim respect is off while AlifePlus loot ownership is on. Disable one of them.
Bought
Stolen
Claimed
This kill belongs to %s, who is too far off to deal with.
You strip a body that %s had claimed. No coin changed hands.
%s scratches his beard. 'Slukhai, stalker. %s RU and we are even. Dobre?'
'No sense spilling blood over a corpse,' says %s. 'Give me %s RU and it is yours.'
%s nods slowly. 'Good. Take what you need, and watch yourself out there.'
'Fair is fair,' says %s. The body is yours now.
%s grins, gold tooth shining. 'Oi, khlopche! %s RU and the stiff is yours, nu!'
'Nu shcho,' sneers %s. 'Hand over %s RU or clear off, before I lose my patience.'
%s snatches the money and laughs. 'Dobre, dobre! It is yours.'
'Knew you had a head on your shoulders,' %s spits. 'Take it and vanish.'
%s stands straight. 'This kill is Duty salvage. %s RU for the rights. Make it fast.'
'Rules are rules, stalker,' barks %s. 'Pay the %s RU fee and it is logged to you.'
%s gives a curt nod. 'Salvage approved. Carry on.'
'For the good of the Zone,' says %s, stepping aside. It is yours.
%s leans on his rifle. 'Easy, stalker. %s RU and the body is yours. No quarrel here.'
'Nothing is truly free, you know,' grins %s. 'Toss me %s RU and we are even.'
%s waves a hand. 'There you go. The Zone belongs to all of us anyway.'
'Stay free, druzhe,' says %s. The body is yours.
%s checks his PDA. 'This kill is on record. %s RU settles it. Agreed?'
'Clear Sky does not work for nothing,' says %s. '%s RU and the salvage is yours.'
%s logs the trade. 'Done. The body is released to you.'
'A pleasure dealing with a professional,' %s nods. It is yours.
%s does not blink. 'Everything has a price. This one is %s RU. No bargaining.'
'You pay, you take,' says %s flatly. '%s RU. Decide.'
%s pockets the roubles without a word. The body is yours.
'Contract closed,' says %s. 'It is yours. We are finished here.'
%s lowers his voice. 'This is restricted salvage, civilian. %s RU and I saw no one.'
'Property of the military,' growls %s. 'But %s RU makes it vanish.'
%s hides the money quickly. 'Move along. You were never here.'
'Off the record,' mutters %s. 'Take it and go.'
%s adjusts his mask. 'The specimen, yes? %s RU toward our research, and it is yours.'
'For the funding only, you understand,' says %s. '%s RU, if you please.'
%s makes a note. 'Specimen released. Do handle it with care.'
'Most intriguing choice,' murmurs %s. It is yours.
%s stares through you. 'The Monolith permits it. %s RU as tribute, and the flesh is yours.'
'The crystal names a price,' intones %s. '%s RU. Obey it.'
%s lowers his head. 'The Monolith is appeased. Take it.'
'Go now, servant of the Zone,' breathes %s. It is yours.
%s twitches, blade in hand. 'Hroshi first, freak! %s RU, or you go on the pile!'
'Ha! You want it?' cackles %s. '%s RU, and maybe you live.'
%s grabs the money, grinning. 'Take it and run, little stalker, run!'
'Your lucky day,' giggles %s. 'It is yours, freak.'
%s rasps behind his mask. 'The Zone gives and takes. %s RU, and it is yours.'
'Sin forgets no debt,' says %s. 'Pay %s RU and the kill is released.'
%s makes a slow sign. 'The debt is settled. Take it.'
'Walk the true path, stalker,' whispers %s. It is yours.
%s regards you coldly. 'This asset is logged to my unit. %s RU clears it. Quickly.'
'Need to know, stalker,' says %s. 'You need to pay %s RU. That is all you need to know.'
%s pockets the cash, expression unchanged. 'Cleared. The body is yours.'
'Transaction recorded,' says %s. 'We were never here. Move.'
Loot Claim Buyout

Buy claimed bodies from the NPCs guarding them. Human bodies are priced by rank, mutants by difficulty and where they died; both are adjusted by your reputation with the claiming faction plus a small random factor.
Enable buying claimed bodies
When off, the original claim behaviour is restored (you are only warned off claimed bodies).
Interaction mode

At the body: the buy prompt appears when you USE the corpse (the claimer must be nearby). Talk to the killer: using the corpse only tells you who claimed it; you must find that NPC and USE them to buy.
At the body
Talk to the killer
Base price (RU)
Baseline cost before rank/difficulty, reputation and random modifiers are applied.
Minimum price (RU)
The price will never drop below this floor, no matter the modifiers.
Human rank influence
How strongly a human body's rank changes the price. 0 ignores rank; higher means veterans/masters cost much more and novices much less.
Mutant difficulty influence
How strongly a mutant's difficulty changes the price. 0 ignores difficulty; higher means a flesh stays cheap while a chimera or pseudogiant costs a fortune.
Map danger influence

How strongly the map a body was killed on changes its price. 0 ignores location; higher means kills in Cordon are cheap and kills in Red Forest or the CNPP are worth far more. Applies to mutants, and to humans if the option below is on.
Apply map danger to human bodies too
When on, the map danger multiplier also affects human corpse prices, not just mutants. Uses the same Map danger influence slider above.
Reputation divisor
Larger values make your reputation with the claiming faction matter less. Smaller values make friends much cheaper and rivals much pricier.
Reputation cap
Maximum discount or surcharge from reputation. 0.5 means at most 50% cheaper if they like you, or 50% pricier if they do not.
Your rank affects price
When on, your own rank changes the price. A rookie gets bullied and pays more, a veteran or higher is respected and pays less.
Your rank influence
How strongly your rank moves the price. At 1.0 a rookie pays about 20% more and a legend about 45% less, 0 ignores your rank.
Random spread
Size of the random price jitter. 0.15 means the final price varies by up to plus or minus 15%. The roll is fixed per body so it can't be rolled again.
Refuse to sell goodwill
If the claiming faction's goodwill toward you is at or below this value, they refuse to deal and warn you off (original behaviour).
Deal range (m)

How close the claiming NPC must be to deal: within range you get the buy prompt (or the "go find them" hint in talk mode). Beyond it the claimer is too far to stop you. See the free looting option below.
Loot for free when the claimer is far
When on, if the claiming NPC is farther than the deal range you can loot the body for free instead of buying it. When off, the base mod's "too far" behaviour applies.
Free looting can alert the faction
When on, looting a claimed body for free has a chance to alert nearby members of that faction to your location, and they turn hostile and come for you.
Alert chance
Probability that free looting gives you away. 0 = never, 1 = always.
Alert radius (m)
How close faction members must be to notice you and turn hostile when you loot for free.
NPCs respect claims

When on, an NPC will not loot a body that a different NPC claimed by killing it. Needs More Aggressive NPC Looting. Disabled automatically while AlifePlus loot ownership is on, since that is a competing system.
Owner can loot its kill
When on, the NPC that made the kill can still loot its own body. Turn off to keep every claimed body untouched until you buy it.
Use PDA notifications
When on, buy/steal/find-the-killer messages are shown as persistent PDA notifications carrying the NPC's name and face, so you know who to go to, instead of brief on-screen text.
Debug logging
Writes diagnostics to appdata\logs\npc_loot_buy_dbg.log and shows them on screen, to troubleshoot why a buy prompt does or does not appear. Leave off during normal play.
